why_this_file_exists: SOL PLAN 4 item 4 (gap_search.py) turns a coverage cell into an EVIDENCE REQUIREMENT and
generates a DIFFERENT query family per gap type. The PRIMARY query vocabulary always comes from
the compiled research_contract (core_concepts.aliases, subject_axis values, outcome_dimensions,
method_designs, required_contrasts, source_policy). THIS FILE holds only the EVIDENCE-SHAPE
vocabulary -- the words that describe the SHAPE of empirical evidence (a null, a moderator, a
counter-finding, a synthesis) and the adapter fan-out per gap. That vocabulary is methodology-
general: a null in a drug trial, a wage study, and a liability analysis all say 'no significant
effect'. A DOMAIN CHANGE (AI -> Parkinson's -> ADAS liability) edits the CONTRACT, never this file
and never gap_search.py. If a genre needs its own shape words (legal: 'circuit split', 'overruled'),
ADD A ROW to evidence_shape.by_genre -- that is a DATA edit, which is the whole point.

by_genre._comment: OPTIONAL genre-specific shape words, merged over the general lists above when the
by_genre._comment2: contract.genre matches. This is the DATA seam for legal/clinical shape vocabulary.

adapters._comment: Backend fan-out per gap type. These are ADAPTER NAMES the acquisition layer (acquisition.py
adapters._comment2: Acquirer.plan_route + the fetchers) already speaks. gap_search EMITS a plan; it does not
adapters._comment3: open a socket. discovery/access/etc. differ so a gap never re-issues a copy of the broad query.

saturation._comment: OPERATIONAL saturation, not a claim of exhaustive recall. A cell may close as THIN / declare
saturation._comment2: a scoped absence only after this many DISTINCT query families over this many adapters have run,
saturation._comment3: citation-chasing and version-pursuit were attempted, and duplicates were collapsed.

_comment: RECENCY BINDING (Sol plan 4 item 3). Turns CONTRACT DATA into the two keys scripts/recency.py needs:
a CLAIM KIND (a row in recency_frontier_profile.yaml:claim_recency_policy -- it decides the LANES,
foundation-only vs frontier, and whether age is penalized at all) and an optional FRONTIER BOUNDARY.
THE DEFAULTS MUST NEVER REQUIRE A BOUNDARY. A claim kind with boundary_required=true (e.g.
current_adoption_rate) is a HARD ERROR when no boundary is named -- recency._policy_for raises, and
rightly so. So the genre rows below name only boundary-FREE kinds, and we UPGRADE to the
boundary-requiring kind ONLY when boundary_by_concept actually resolves one. A generic literature
review therefore gets a relative 24-month frontier window; the AI question gets the 2023 epoch.
GENERALITY: a FOUNDATIONAL_THEORY question resolves to lanes=[foundation], penalize_age=false -- a
1979 theorem is NEVER stale and NEVER a RECENCY_GAP. Legal -> statute_current_text; clinical ->
clinical_conclusion; thin -> thin_evidence_current. A NEW DOMAIN IS A ROW HERE, NEVER A CODE EDIT.
_boundary_comment: concept KEY (contract.core_concepts[].key) -> frontier_boundaries key. The contract names the
concept; this row names the epoch after which the citation graph cannot see the work.
